acts.title: Health Services Act
acts.summary: Establishes Health Council, Regional Hospitals Boards, and Hospital Committees for governance of public health services.

acts.title: Medical Ordinance
acts.summary: Governs registration and regulation of medical practitioners, dentists, midwives, pharmacists, para-medical assistants, and nurses through the Sri Lanka Medical Council and College Council.

acts.title: Medical Wants Ordinance
acts.summary: Consolidates the law relating to medical wants of labourers in planting districts. Establishes Medical Wants Committee, District Medical Officer system, and export duty-based funding for estate medical care.

acts.title: Mental Disease Ordinance
acts.summary: Regulates the care, treatment, and custody of persons of unsound mind and administration of mental health institutions.

acts.title: National Health Development Fund Act
acts.summary: Establishes a fund and Board of Trustees for the development and improvement of health services in Sri Lanka.

acts.title: Nursing Homes (Regulations) Act
acts.summary: Provides for registration and regulation of nursing homes to ensure standards of care and safety.

acts.title: Poisons, Opium & Dangerous Drugs Ordinance
acts.summary: Controls the import, export, manufacture, sale, and possession of poisons, opium, and dangerous drugs.

acts.title: Private Medical Institutions (Registration) Act
acts.summary: Requires registration of private medical institutions and establishes regulatory framework for private healthcare.

acts.title: Sri Lanka Nurses' Council Act
acts.summary: Establishes the Sri Lanka Nurses' Council for registration, regulation, and standards of nursing practice.

acts.title: Transplantation of Human Tissues Act
acts.summary: Regulates the removal and transplantation of human tissues and organs, including consent and authorization.

acts.title: Food Act
acts.summary: Provides for the regulation of the manufacture, import, sale, and distribution of food to ensure safety and standards.

acts.title: Sri Jayewardenepura General Hospital Board Act
acts.summary: Establishes a Board for the management and administration of Sri Jayewardenepura General Hospital.

acts.title: Vijaya Kumaratunga Memorial Hospital Board Act
acts.summary: Establishes a 7-member Board (majority nominated by VK Memorial Foundation) for the management and administration of Vijaya Kumaratunga Memorial Hospital at Seeduwa. Creates a Committee of Management and dedicated Fund. Hospital operational since 1999 with 137 beds.

acts.title: National Authority on Tobacco and Alcohol Act
acts.summary: Establishes NATA as a 14-member body corporate (9 ex-officio from 6 ministries + Police + Excise + NDDCB, plus 5 ministerially-appointed experts) to combat tobacco and alcohol harm. 45 sections in 2 Parts. Key prohibitions: sale to under-21s, comprehensive advertising/sponsorship ban, public smoking ban. Sri Lanka was the first Asian country to ratify the WHO FCTC. Amended by Act No. 3 of 2015 mandating 80% pictorial health warnings.

acts.title: 1990 Suwaseriya Foundation Act
acts.summary: Establishes the 1990 Suwaseriya Foundation as a body corporate with a 7-member Board (4 appointed by President + 3 ex-officio from Health, Finance, Police). 28 sections. Provides free pre-hospital care ambulance services island-wide through 297+ ambulances covering all 9 provinces. Transitioned from GVK EMRI Lanka under a USD 22.58 million Indian Government grant. No amendments.

acts.title: National Medicines Regulatory Authority Act
acts.summary: Establishes NMRA as a self-funded 13-member body corporate (3 ex-officio + 10 appointed) succeeding the CDDA. 147 sections in 7 Chapters. Regulates medicines, medical devices, borderline products, and cosmetics. Creates MEC, MDEC, BPEC evaluation committees, Pricing Committee, NMQAL, and 4 regulatory divisions. Implements the Senaka Bibile National Drug Policy. No amendments; repeals CDDA Act No. 27 of 1980.

acts.title: Ayurveda Act
acts.summary: Establishes 3 statutory bodies (Department of Ayurveda, Ayurveda Medical Council, Ayurveda Education & Hospital Board) for the development and regulation of 4 traditional medicine systems: Ayurveda, Siddha, Unani, and Desiya Chikitsa. ~77+ sections in 6 Parts. Commissioner-General heads the Department and chairs the Council. 3 amendments (1969, 1977, 2023) — the 2023 modernization added herbal garden registration and massage therapist regulation.

acts.title: Homoeopathy Act
acts.summary: Established the Homoeopathic Council (body corporate, S.2) for registration and regulation of homoeopathic practitioners, with a dedicated Homoeopathic Fund (S.39). Never amended. Repealed in its entirety by Section 50 of the Homoeopathy Act, No. 10 of 2016 after 46 years, following administrative collapse of the Council.
